ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, DEFENDANT DOMCO PRODUCTS TEXAS INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT DOMCO PRODUCTS TEXAS INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 36 THROUGH 40 BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES ARE INSUFFICIENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 4.1 AND 6.1 THROUGH 6.7 BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS NEVER SERVED ANY RESPONSES AT ALL TO THOSE INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFFS HAVE 15 DAYS TO SERVE RESPONSES. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DEEM ADMITTED REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 15 THROUGH 30 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO TIMELY RESPOND TO THOSE REQUESTS AND SUBSEQUENTLY NEVER SERVED RESPONSES IN SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH C.C.P. SECTION 2033.220. (C.C.P. SECTION 2033.280(B), (C).) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS OF $1979.50 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION AS TO ALL OTHER DISCOVERY REQUESTS IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT, AS DEFENDANT'S REPLY DID NOT SUGGEST THAT ANYTHING BUT THE ABOVE-REFERENCED DISCOVERY REMAINED AT ISSUE. Off Calendar Nov-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT RHEEM MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: J. Bruce Jackson, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP 775-1206 for pltfs & opposing parties; Malcolm D. Donaldson, Esq. of Vasquez Estrada & Conway LLP 453-0555 for deft & moving party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Rheem's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Plaintiffs' False Representation cause of action (issue 1) and claim for punitive damages (issue 2) is denied in part and granted in part. Defendant did not sustain its initial burden on the False Representation cause of action. Therefore, the Court denies the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to False Representation. Defendant did sustain its initial burden on the punitive damages issues. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Defendant is liable for punitive damages. Therefore, the Court grants the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter Not Reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant John K. Bice Company Inc. To Verify Its Written Discovery Responses And Deeming Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Young S. Lee of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Jim Kelly, et al.
Jennifer Thai of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant
/ opposing party John K. Bice Company Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS JIM KELLY, ET AL.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING DEFENDANT JOHN K. BICE COMPANY INC. TO VERIFY ITS WRITTEN DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND DEEMING PLAINTIFFS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ADMITTED; AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for an order requiring defendant John K. Bice Comapny Inc. to verify its responses to written discovery and deeming plaintiffs' requests for admission admitted is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant's timely answers to plaintiffs' requests for admission will not be deemed admitted. Both plaintiffs' and defendant's requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Show Cause For Failure To Serve Or In The Alternative Motion For Dismissal And Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Young S. Lee of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Jim Kelly, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT VAN ARSDALE HARRIS LUMBER COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR FAILURE TO SERVE OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR DISMISSAL AND AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing defendant Van Arsdale Harris Lumber Company Inc.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(b)(1) is denied. Although Plaintiffs failed to serve defendant within two years of commencing the personal injury action, Defendant already made a general appearance in the present action by appearing at depositions, curing defects in service. There seems to be some dispute about whether defendant answered the personal injury complaint, although the court does not see that any such answer was ever filed. Further, the court does not find prejudice to defendant. As this is a discretionary dismissal statute, the court refuses to exercise its discretion to dismiss defendant. Defendant's and plaintiffs' requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery, To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Domco Products Texas Inc.
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Jim Kelly, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT DOMCO PRODUCTS TEXAS INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Domco Products Texas Inc.'s motion to compel further responses is granted as to special interrogatories 36 through 40 because plaintiffs' responses are insufficient. Defendant's motion to compel is granted as to form interrogatories 4.1 and 6.1 through 6.7 because plaintiffs never served any responses at all to those interrogatories. Plaintiffs have 15 days to serve responses. Defendant's motion to deem admitted requests for admission nos. 15 through 30 is granted. Plaintiffs failed to timely respond to those requests and subsequently never served responses in substantial compliance with C.C.P. section 2033.220. (C.C.P. section 2033.280(b), (c).) Defendant's request for sanctions of $1979.50 is granted. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate substantial justification. Defendant's motion as to all other discovery requests is off calendar as moot, as defendant's reply did not suggest that anything but the above-referenced discovery remained at issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Monica Williams of Tucker Ellis & West LLP (415-671-2210) for Carrier Corporation Defendant and Moving Party / Bruce Jackson of Keller Fishback & Jackson LLP (818-342-7442) for Jim Kelly Plaintiff and Opposing Party

ARGUED AND COURT ADOPTS IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S motion to strike plaintiffs' prayer for punitive damages is granted with 10 days leave to amend. The court did not consider plaintiffs' opposition. Pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), plaintiffs' opposition was due by August 11. Plaintiffs did not file their opposition until August 19, only 3 court days before this hearing. Plaintiffs' opposition and attorney declaration failed to demonstrate any good cause for the court to consider the late-filed opposition. See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019). Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Teri L. Jackson, CSR: T. Willat-Harrison, #4609 (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Monica Williams of Tucker Ellis & West LLP (415-671-2210) for Carrier Corporation Defendant and Moving Party / Bruce Jackson of Keller Fishback & Jackson LLP (818-342-7442) for Jim Kelly Plaintiff and Opposing Party

NOT ARGUED - COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION's DEMURRER to the first, second, third, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action in plaintiffs' second amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs' causes of action contain allegations sufficient to state the bases for their claims against defendant. This includes sufficient product identification under Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, and sufficient allegations as to the false representation (Restatement 402-B) and intentional tort (Civil Code Sections 1708, 1709 and 1710) causes of action. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Based on defendant's representation to the court on August 24, defendant is not contesting this tentative ruling. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Teri L. Jackson, CSR: T. Willat-Harrison, #4609 (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on plaintiffs Jim Kelly and Mary Kelly's Motion for Leave to File an Amended Complaint.

Sharon L. Clisham, Esq. appeared for defendants B.R. Funston & Co. and D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.

Order After Hearing was submitted to and signed by the Court.
